hypothesis: baseline reproduces the tuned mar28 defaults on the current system torch stack and may shift the runtime frontier

hypothesis: adding one layer will use the current artifact headroom more effectively than it harms fixed-budget step count
notes: 676 steps in 600.5s; artifact 13.67 MB; zlib 1.4424; TTT eval 885s; depth alone lost too many steps

hypothesis: 10 layers can beat the 9-layer baseline if a smaller batch recovers enough optimizer steps under the same 600s budget
notes: 878 steps in 600.6s; artifact 14.52 MB; zlib 1.4259; TTT eval 886s; depth was step-starved rather than fundamentally bad

hypothesis: after depth-plus-step recovery wins at 196608 tokens, another batch reduction to 131072 will improve further before gradient noise dominates
notes: 1208 steps in 600.1s; artifact 15.82 MB; zlib 1.4270; only a 0.0003 improvement over the 196608 run, so likely near the flat part of the frontier

hypothesis: with 10 layers, reducing KV heads from 2 to 1 can recover attention speed with less batch-noise penalty than pushing batch tokens down to 131072
notes: 896 steps in 600.3s; artifact 13.40 MB; zlib 1.4319; TTT eval 861s; cheaper attention recovered some speed but quality loss outweighed it

hypothesis: at fixed 10-layer depth, a thinner `MLP_MULT=1` may free enough compute to beat the current `MLP_MULT=2` balance
notes: 1044 steps in 600.4s; artifact 11.27 MB; zlib 1.4337; TTT eval 754s; thinner MLP bought more steps and much more artifact headroom, but the capacity loss still outweighed the speed gain

hypothesis: at fixed 10-layer depth, a wider `MLP_MULT=3` may improve quality enough to justify fewer steps and a larger artifact
notes: 746 steps in 600.8s; artifact 17.43 MB, over the 16 MB cap; zlib 1.4388; TTT eval 1032s; wider MLP was worse than the anchor even before accounting for invalid submission size

hypothesis: reallocating width into one more layer via `11L / MLP1` may beat the current `10L / MLP2` balance
notes: 948 steps in 600.2s; artifact 12.03 MB; zlib 1.4375; TTT eval 823s; deeper but thinner was cleaner than pure width, but still clearly worse than the `10L / MLP2` anchor

hypothesis: trading one layer for a wider MLP via `9L / MLP3` may be the viable width-oriented frontier
notes: 829 steps in 600.1s; artifact 16.18 MB, slightly over the cap; zlib 1.4279; TTT eval 933s; this was the strongest width branch so far and materially better than `10L / MLP3`, but it still lost to the `10L / MLP2` anchor and missed size validity

hypothesis: the `9L / MLP3` near-miss may need more optimizer steps rather than a different width-depth ratio
notes: 1155 steps in 600.5s; artifact 17.68 MB, well over the cap; zlib 1.4257; TTT eval 933s; more steps flipped the score and produced the best raw `val_bpb` seen so far, but the compression story got worse, so it is still invalid as a challenge frontier

hypothesis: a mild `MODEL_DIM` trim may keep most of the `9L / MLP3 / 131072` gain while recovering challenge-valid size
notes: 1146 steps in 600.2s; artifact 15.64 MB, now valid; zlib 1.4324; TTT eval 926s; the mild dim trim solved the size problem cleanly but gave back too much score to challenge the current valid frontier

hypothesis: most of the width-branch gain may survive if the model keeps `9` layers and `131072` batch but drops from `MLP_MULT=3` to `MLP_MULT=2`
notes: 1349 steps in 600.4s; artifact 14.73 MB, valid; zlib 1.4200; TTT eval 801s; one MLP-notch trim kept the width-step interaction while fixing size and produced the new best valid frontier

hypothesis: a stronger global trim to `DIM448` may recover most of the `9L / MLP3 / 131072` score while staying clearly inside the size cap
notes: 1287 steps in 600.2s; artifact 14.19 MB, valid; zlib 1.4273; TTT eval 856s; stronger dim trimming recovered far more score than `DIM480`, but it still trailed the new `9L / MLP2` frontier

hypothesis: one less layer may be a cleaner size cut than dropping the third MLP notch inside each block
notes: 1304 steps in 600.1s; artifact 16.29 MB, still invalid; zlib 1.4274; TTT eval 836s; dropping one layer preserved decent quality, but it neither beat `9L / MLP2` nor solved the size problem

hypothesis: two light cuts together, one layer plus `DIM480`, may preserve score better than a single aggressive trim
notes: 1292 steps in 600.4s; artifact 14.42 MB, valid; zlib 1.4267; TTT eval 829s; the combined light cuts beat the other fallback trims, but still trailed the `9L / MLP2` winner

hypothesis: the new `9L / MLP2` winner may still be meaningfully step-limited at `131072` tokens and improve further at `98304`
notes: 1718 steps in 600.1s; artifact 15.48 MB, valid; zlib 1.4091; TTT eval 801s; more steps produced a major frontier jump, so the new winner was still under-trained
